name: Porta Cabin House
description:
A porta cabin house is a factory-built living unit — a porta cabin finished to residential standards so it works as a home, not a site office. SAMAN builds it as a 30 × 10 ft steel-and-wood structure with insulated walls, a wired interior and a bathroom provision, delivered ready and priced at a fixed ₹3,65,000.

For years "porta cabin" meant a contractor's site box. Today the same build, fitted for living, is how many buyers in India put up a quick home on a plot, a farm, or a rooftop — without months of brick-and-mortar work.

₹3,65,000, Fully Fitted — The 30×10 Porta Cabin House Build Sheet

A porta cabin house set up as a home on a private plot.

Most sellers quote a porta cabin house price by the square foot, which leaves you guessing at the real total. SAMAN quotes one number for a finished unit: ₹3,65,000 for the 30 × 10 × 9 ft house (regular ₹3,75,000). That covers the mild-steel frame, insulated sandwich-panel walls, a single-skin steel roof, internal electrical wiring with switchboard, and provision for an attached bathroom. Flooring, doors and windows are fitted before dispatch. Eight exterior colour options are available, and the unit is configured to your room plan.

Can You Live in It Year-Round? Insulation, Comfort and Daily Use

Inside year-round — insulated and AC-ready for Indian summers.

The honest question buyers ask is whether a steel-framed unit is comfortable through an Indian summer and monsoon. It is — because the walls and roof use insulated sandwich panels rather than bare sheet, which keeps internal temperatures steadier and makes the unit AC-ready. Inside the 300 sq ft floor you can lay out a bedroom, a compact kitchen and a bathroom, or open it up as a single studio. Porta cabin house design is set at the order stage — partition positions, window placement and finish — so the same shell serves as a weekend home, a caretaker's residence, or long-stay accommodation.

Where It Goes — Plot, Farm, Terrace or Site — and What the Ground Needs

On a farm — a quick, relocatable home without a foundation.

A porta cabin house does not need a conventional foundation. It sits on a level, compacted surface or a simple plinth, which is why it goes up in days rather than months and can be lifted and relocated later if your plans change. That makes it practical on a vacant plot, a farm, an existing rooftop with adequate load capacity, or a project site. We deliver and place units across India from our Bangalore and Delhi NCR facilities. Local rules differ, so confirm any municipal permission for a habitable structure on your specific plot before installation — a step buyers often miss.

Porta Cabin House or Portable Cabin House — Which Term, Which Build

Buyers reach this product using two words for nearly the same idea — plus a third that is genuinely different. The quick distinction:

Porta cabin house (this page) Portable cabin house Container house
Known as "porta cabin" home "portable cabin" home shipping-container home
Build Steel & wood, cabin form Steel & wood, cabin form ISO container shell
From ₹3,65,000 ₹2,56,000 container-based, higher
Pick when you think "porta cabin" you prefer "portable cabin" wording you want the container look

On a rooftop — extra living space on an existing terrace with adequate load capacity.

If you tend to call it a portable cabin, the portable cabin house build covers the same residential idea in that wording. If you specifically want the shipping-container form factor, that is a separate product line. This page is the porta-cabin-form home.

Part of SAMAN's Prefabricated House Range

Part of SAMAN's prefabricated house range — single homes through to team bunkhouses.

This porta cabin house is one unit in SAMAN's wider prefabricated house range, which spans single-family homes and site-living accommodation. If you need to house a small team rather than a household, a prefabricated bunkhouse gives you multiple beds and shared facilities in the same factory-built, relocatable format.

Porta Cabin House — FAQs

What is a porta cabin house?

A porta cabin house is a porta cabin built to residential standards — insulated, wired and bathroom-ready — so it can be lived in as a home rather than used as a site office.

What is the price of a porta cabin house in India?

SAMAN's 30 × 10 ft porta cabin house is ₹3,65,000 (regular ₹3,75,000) as a fully fitted unit. The final figure depends on layout, finish and delivery distance.

Does a porta cabin house need a foundation or municipal approval?

No conventional foundation is needed — a level surface or a simple plinth is enough. Permission rules for a habitable structure vary by location, so check with your local authority for your plot.

What's the difference between a porta cabin house and a portable cabin house?

They are the same residential idea in different words. This page serves buyers who search "porta cabin"; the portable cabin house build serves those who use "portable cabin" wording.

Get a Free Quote for Your Porta Cabin House

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
